sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: I thank you, sir.

sentences.speaker: SECOND FISHERMAN
sentences.text: Hark you, my friend; you said you could not beg.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: I did but crave.

sentences.speaker: SECOND FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
But crave! Then I'll turn craver too, and so I
shall 'scape whipping.

sentences.speaker: SECOND FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
But crave! Then I'll turn craver too, and so I
shall 'scape whipping.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: Why, are all your beggars whipped, then?

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: Why, are all your beggars whipped, then?

sentences.speaker: SECOND FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
O, not all, my friend, not all; for if all your
beggars were whipped, I would wish no better office
than to be beadle. But, master, I'll go draw up the
net.

sentences.speaker: SECOND FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
O, not all, my friend, not all; for if all your
beggars were whipped, I would wish no better office
than to be beadle. But, master, I'll go draw up the
net.

Exit with Third Fisherman

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
[Aside] How well this honest mirth becomes their
labour!

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text: Hark you, sir, do you know where ye are?

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: Not well.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Why, I'll tell you: this is called Pentapolis, and
our king the good Simonides.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Why, I'll tell you: this is called Pentapolis, and
our king the good Simonides.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: The good King Simonides, do you call him.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Ay, sir; and he deserves so to be called for his
peaceable reign and good government.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Ay, sir; and he deserves so to be called for his
peaceable reign and good government.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
He is a happy king, since he gains from his subjects
the name of good by his government. How far is his
court distant from this shore?

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
He is a happy king, since he gains from his subjects
the name of good by his government. How far is his
court distant from this shore?

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, half a day's journey: and I'll tell
you, he hath a fair daughter, and to-morrow is her
birth-day; and there are princes and knights come
from all parts of the world to just and tourney for her
love.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, half a day's journey: and I'll tell
you, he hath a fair daughter, and to-morrow is her
birth-day; and there are princes and knights come
from all parts of the world to just and tourney for her
love.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, half a day's journey: and I'll tell
you, he hath a fair daughter, and to-morrow is her
birth-day; and there are princes and knights come
from all parts of the world to just and tourney for her
love.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Were my fortunes equal to my desires, I could wish
to make one there.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
O, sir, things must be as they may; and what a man
cannot get, he may lawfully deal for—his wife's soul.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
O, sir, things must be as they may; and what a man
cannot get, he may lawfully deal for—his wife's soul.
Re-enter Second and Third Fishermen, drawing up a net

sentences.speaker: SECOND FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Help, master, help! here's a fish hangs in the net,
like a poor man's right in the law; 'twill hardly
come out. Ha! bots on't, 'tis come at last, and
'tis turned to a rusty armour.

come out. Ha! bots on't, 'tis come at last, and

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
An armour, friends! I pray you, let me see it.
Thanks, fortune, yet, that, after all my crosses,
Thou givest me somewhat to repair myself;
And though it was mine own, part of my heritage,
Which my dead father did bequeath to me.
With this strict charge, even as he left his life,
'Keep it, my Pericles; it hath been a shield
Twixt me and death;'— and pointed to this brace;—
'For that it saved me, keep it; in like necessity—
The which the gods protect thee from!— may
defend thee.'
It kept where I kept, I so dearly loved it;
Till the rough seas, that spare not any man,
Took it in rage, though calm'd have given't again:
I thank thee for't: my shipwreck now's no ill,
Since I have here my father's gift in's will.

An armour, friends! I pray you, let me see it.
Thanks, fortune, yet, that, after all my crosses,
Thou givest me somewhat to repair myself;
And though it was mine own, part of my heritage,
Which my dead father did bequeath to me.
With this strict charge, even as he left his life,
'Keep it, my Pericles; it hath been a shield
Twixt me and death;'— and pointed to this brace;—
'For that it saved me, keep it; in like necessity—
The which the gods protect thee from!— may
defend thee.'
It kept where I kept, I so dearly loved it;
Till the rough seas, that spare not any man,
Took it in rage, though calm'd have given't again:
I thank thee for't: my shipwreck now's no ill,
Since I have here my father's gift in's will.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text: What mean you, sir?

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
PERICLES
PERICLES
PERICLES
PERICLES
FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text: Why, wilt thou tourney for the lady?

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: I'll show the virtue I have borne in arms.

sentences.speaker: FIRST FISHERMAN
sentences.text: Why, do 'e take it, and the gods give thee good on't!

sentences.speaker: SECOND FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Ay, but hark you, my friend; 'twas we that made up
this garment through the rough seams of the waters:
there are certain condolements, certain vails. I
hope, sir, if you thrive, you'll remember from
whence you had it.

sentences.speaker: SECOND FISHERMAN
sentences.text:
Ay, but hark you, my friend; 'twas we that made up

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Believe 't, I will.
By your furtherance I am clothed in steel;
And, spite of all the rapture of the sea,
This jewel holds his building on my arm:
Unto thy value I will mount myself
Upon a courser, whose delightful steps
Shall make the gazer joy to see him tread.
Only, my friend, I yet am unprovided
Of a pair of bases.

We'll sure provide: thou shalt have my best gown to
make thee a pair; and I'll bring thee to the court
myself.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Then honour be but a goal to my will,
This day I'll rise, or else add ill to ill.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES

Enter SIMONIDES, THAISA, Lords, and Attendants

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: Are the knights ready to begin the triumph?

sentences.speaker: FIRST LORD
sentences.text:
They are, my liege;
And stay your coming to present themselves.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Return them, we are ready; and our daughter,
In honour of whose birth these triumphs are,
Sits here, like beauty's child, whom nature gat
For men to see, and seeing wonder at.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
It pleaseth you, my royal father, to express
My commendations great, whose merit's less.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
It's fit it should be so; for princes are
A model which heaven makes like to itself:
As jewels lose their glory if neglected,
So princes their renowns if not respected.
'Tis now your honour, daughter, to explain
The labour of each knight in his device.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: Who is the first that doth prefer himself?

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
A knight of Sparta, my renowned father;
And the device he bears upon his shield
Is a black Ethiope reaching at the sun
The word, 'Lux tua vita mihi.'

SIMONIDES

sentences.speaker: THAISA

A burning torch that's turned upside down;

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES

Which shows that beauty hath his power and will,
Which can as well inflame as it can kill.

And what's

FIRST LORD
sentences.text:
He had need mean better than his outward show
Can any way speak in his just commend;
For by his rusty outside he appears
To have practised more the whipstock than the lance.

sentences.speaker: FIRST LORD

sentences.speaker: SECOND LORD
sentences.text:
He well may be a stranger, for he comes
To an honour'd triumph strangely furnished.

sentences.speaker: THIRD LORD
sentences.text:
And on set purpose let his armour rust
Until this day, to scour it in the dust.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Opinion's but a fool, that makes us scan
The outward habit by the inward man.
But stay, the knights are coming: we will withdraw
Into the gallery.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES

Great shouts within and all cry, "The mean knight!" Enter SIMONIDES, THAISA, Lords, Attendants, and Knights, from tilting
Great shouts within and all cry, "The mean knight!" Enter SIMONIDES, THAISA, Lords, Attendants, and Knights, from tilting

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Knights,
To say you're welcome were superfluous.
To place upon the volume of your deeds,
As in a title-page, your worth in arms,
Were more than you expect, or more than's fit,
Since every worth in show commends itself.
Prepare for mirth, for mirth becomes a feast:
You are princes and my guests.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
THAISA
PERICLES
sentences.text: 'Tis more by fortune, lady, than by merit.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Call it by what you will, the day is yours;
And here, I hope, is none that envies it.
In framing an artist, art hath thus decreed,
To make some good, but others to exceed;
And you are her labour'd scholar. Come, queen o'
the feast,—
For, daughter, so you are,— here take your place:
Marshal the rest, as they deserve their grace.

sentences.speaker: KNIGHTS
sentences.text: We are honour'd much by good Simonides.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Your presence glads our days: honour we love;
For who hates honour hates the gods above.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Your presence glads our days: honour we love;
For who hates honour hates the gods above.

sentences.speaker: MARSHAL
sentences.text: Sir, yonder is your place.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: Some other is more fit.

sentences.speaker: FIRST KNIGHT
sentences.text:
Contend not, sir; for we are gentlemen
That neither in our hearts nor outward eyes
Envy the great nor do the low despise.

sentences.speaker: FIRST KNIGHT
sentences.text:

That neither in our hearts nor outward eyes
Envy the great nor do the low despise.

sentences.speaker: FIRST KNIGHT
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: You are right courteous knights.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: Sit, sir, sit.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
By Jove, I wonder, that is king of thoughts,
These cates resist me, she but thought upon.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
By Juno, that is queen of marriage,
All viands that I eat do seem unsavoury.
Wishing him my meat. Sure, he's a gallant gentleman.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
SIMONIDES
THAISA
sentences.text: To me he seems like diamond to glass.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Yon king's to me like to my father's picture,
Which tells me in that glory once he was;
Had princes sit, like stars, about his throne,
And he the sun, for them to reverence;
None that beheld him, but, like lesser lights,
Did vail their crowns to his supremacy:
Where now his son's like a glow-worm in the night,
The which hath fire in darkness, none in light:
Whereby I see that Time's the king of men,
He's both their parent, and he is their grave,
And gives them what he will, not what they crave.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: What, are you merry, knights?

sentences.speaker: KNIGHTS
sentences.text: Who can be other in this royal presence?

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Here, with a cup that's stored unto the brim,—
As you do love, fill to your mistress' lips,—
We drink this health to you.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Here, with a cup that's stored unto the brim,—
As you do love, fill to your mistress' lips,—
We drink this health to you.

sentences.speaker: KNIGHTS
sentences.text: We thank your grace.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Yet pause awhile:
Yon knight doth sit too melancholy,
As if the entertainment in our court
Had not a show might countervail his worth.
Note it not you, Thaisa?

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Yet pause awhile:
Yon knight doth sit too melancholy,
As if the entertainment in our court
Had not a show might countervail his worth.
Note it not you, Thaisa?

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
What is it
To me, my father?

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
O, attend, my daughter:
Princes in this should live like gods above,
Who freely give to every one that comes
To honour them:
And princes not doing so are like to gnats,
Which make a sound, but kill'd are wonder'd at.
Therefore to make his entrance more sweet,
Here, say we drink this standing-bowl of wine to him.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
Alas, my father, it befits not me
Unto a stranger knight to be so bold:
He may my proffer take for an offence,
Since men take women's gifts for impudence.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
How!
Do as I bid you, or you'll move me else.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
How!
Do as I bid you, or you'll move me else.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
[Aside] Now, by the gods, he could not please me
better.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
And furthermore tell him, we desire to know of him,
Of whence he is, his name and parentage.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text: The king my father, sir, has drunk to you.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: I thank him.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text: Wishing it so much blood unto your life.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: I thank both him and you, and pledge him freely.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: I thank both him and you, and pledge him freely.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
And further he desires to know of you,
Of whence you are, your name and parentage.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
PERICLES
PERICLES
PERICLES
THAISA
sentences.text:
He thanks your grace; names himself Pericles,
A gentleman of Tyre,
Who only by misfortune of the seas
Bereft of ships and men, cast on this shore.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Now, by the gods, I pity his misfortune,
And will awake him from his melancholy.
Come, gentlemen, we sit too long on trifles,
And waste the time, which looks for other revels.
Even in your armours, as you are address'd,
Will very well become a soldier's dance.
I will not have excuse, with saying this
Loud music is too harsh for ladies' heads,
Since they love men in arms as well as beds.

Even in your armours, as you are address'd,
Will very well become a soldier's dance.
I will not have excuse, with saying this
Loud music is too harsh for ladies' heads,
Since they love men in arms as well as beds.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Now, by the gods, I pity his misfortune,
And will awake him from his melancholy.
Come, gentlemen, we sit too long on trifles,
And waste the time, which looks for other revels.
Even in your armours, as you are address'd,
Will very well become a soldier's dance.
I will not have excuse, with saying this
Loud music is too harsh for ladies' heads,
Since they love men in arms as well as beds.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
SIMONIDES
PERICLES
sentences.text: In those that practise them they are, my lord.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
O, that's as much as you would be denied
Of your fair courtesy.
The Knights and Ladies dance

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Unclasp, unclasp:
Thanks, gentlemen, to all; all have done well.
[ To PERICLES]
But you the best. Pages and lights, to conduct
These knights unto their several lodgings!
Yours, sir,
We have given order to be next our own.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
SIMONIDES

sentences.text: Exeunt
Enter HELICANUS and ESCANES

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text:
No, Escanes, know this of me,
Antiochus from incest lived not free:
For which, the most high gods not minding longer
To withhold the vengeance that they had in store,
Due to this heinous capital offence,
Even in the height and pride of all his glory,
When he was seated in a chariot
Of an inestimable value, and his daughter with him,
A fire from heaven came and shrivell'd up
Their bodies, even to loathing; for they so stunk,
That all those eyes adored them ere their fall
Scorn now their hand should give them burial.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS

sentences.speaker: ESCANES
sentences.text: 'Twas very strange.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text:
And yet but justice; for though
This king were great, his greatness was no guard
To bar heaven's shaft, but sin had his reward.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text:

To bar heaven's shaft, but sin had his reward.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ESCANES
sentences.text: 'Tis very true.
Enter two or three Lords

sentences.speaker: FIRST LORD
sentences.text:
See, not a man in private conference
Or council has respect with him but he.

sentences.speaker: SECOND LORD
sentences.text: It shall no longer grieve without reproof.

sentences.speaker: THIRD LORD
sentences.text: And cursed be he that will not second it.

sentences.speaker: FIRST LORD
sentences.text: Follow me, then. Lord Helicane, a word.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text: With me? and welcome: happy day, my lords.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text: With me? and welcome: happy day, my lords.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text: With me? and welcome: happy day, my lords.

sentences.speaker: FIRST LORD
sentences.text:
Know that our griefs are risen to the top,
And now at length they overflow their banks.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text: Your griefs! for what? wrong not your prince you love.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text: Your griefs! for what? wrong not your prince you love.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text: Your griefs! for what? wrong not your prince you love.

sentences.speaker: FIRST LORD
FIRST LORD
FIRST LORD
FIRST LORD
SECOND LORD
sentences.text:
Whose death indeed's the strongest in our censure:
And knowing this kingdom is without a head,—
Like goodly buildings left without a roof
Soon fall to ruin,— your noble self,
That best know how to rule and how to reign,
We thus submit unto,— our sovereign.

sentences.speaker: SECOND LORD
sentences.text:
Whose death indeed's the strongest in our censure:
And knowing this kingdom is without a head,—
Like goodly buildings left without a roof
Soon fall to ruin,— your noble self,
That best know how to rule and how to reign,
We thus submit unto,— our sovereign.

sentences.speaker: ALL
sentences.text: Live, noble Helicane!

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text:
For honour's cause, forbear your suffrages:
If that you love Prince Pericles, forbear.
Take I your wish, I leap into the seas,
Where's hourly trouble for a minute's ease.
A twelvemonth longer, let me entreat you to
Forbear the absence of your king:
If in which time expired, he not return,
I shall with aged patience bear your yoke.
But if I cannot win you to this love,
Go search like nobles, like noble subjects,
And in your search spend your adventurous worth;
Whom if you find, and win unto return,
You shall like diamonds sit about his crown.

sentences.speaker: FIRST LORD
FIRST LORD
HELICANUS
sentences.text:
Then you love us, we you, and we'll clasp hands:
When peers thus knit, a kingdom ever stands.

sentences.speaker: HELICANUS
sentences.text:
Then you love us, we you, and we'll clasp hands:
When peers thus knit, a kingdom ever stands.
Exeunt
Enter SIMONIDES, reading a letter, at one door: the Knights meet him

sentences.speaker: FIRST KNIGHT
sentences.text: Good morrow to the good Simonides.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Knights, from my daughter this I let you know,
That for this twelvemonth she'll not undertake
A married life.
Her reason to herself is only known,
Which yet from her by no means can I get.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Knights, from my daughter this I let you know,
That for this twelvemonth she'll not undertake
A married life.
Her reason to herself is only known,
Which yet from her by no means can I get.

sentences.speaker: SECOND KNIGHT
sentences.text: May we not get access to her, my lord?

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
'Faith, by no means; she has so strictly tied
Her to her chamber, that 'tis impossible.
One twelve moons more she'll wear Diana's livery;
This by the eye of Cynthia hath she vow'd
And on her virgin honour will not break it.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
'Faith, by no means; she has so strictly tied
Her to her chamber, that 'tis impossible.
One twelve moons more she'll wear Diana's livery;
This by the eye of Cynthia hath she vow'd
And on her virgin honour will not break it.

sentences.speaker: THIRD KNIGHT
sentences.text: Loath to bid farewell, we take our leaves.
Exeunt Knights

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
So,
They are well dispatch'd; now to my daughter's letter:
She tells me here, she'd wed the stranger knight,
Or never more to view nor day nor light.
'Tis well, mistress; your choice agrees with mine;
I like that well: nay, how absolute she's in't,
Not minding whether I dislike or no!
Well, I do commend her choice;
And will no longer have it be delay'd.
Soft! here he comes: I must dissemble it.

Enter PERICLES

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: All fortune to the good Simonides!

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
To you as much, sir! I am beholding to you
For your sweet music this last night: I do
Protest my ears were never better fed
With such delightful pleasing harmony.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
To you as much, sir! I am beholding to you
For your sweet music this last night: I do
Protest my ears were never better fed
With such delightful pleasing harmony.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
To you as much, sir! I am beholding to you
For your sweet music this last night: I do
Protest my ears were never better fed
With such delightful pleasing harmony.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
It is your grace's pleasure to commend;
Not my desert.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: Sir, you are music's master.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: The worst of all her scholars, my good lord.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Let me ask you one thing:
What do you think of my daughter, sir?

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: A most virtuous princess.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: And she is fair too, is she not?

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: As a fair day in summer, wondrous fair.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
SIMONIDES
SIMONIDES
PERICLES
sentences.text: I am unworthy for her schoolmaster.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: She thinks not so; peruse this writing else.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
[Aside] What's here?
A letter, that she loves the knight of Tyre!
'Tis the king's subtlety to have my life.
O, seek not to entrap me, gracious lord,
A stranger and distressed gentleman,
That never aim'd so high to love your daughter,
But bent all offices to honour her.

A letter, that she loves the knight of Tyre!

O, seek not to entrap me, gracious lord,
A stranger and distressed gentleman,

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Thou hast bewitch'd my daughter, and thou art
A villain.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
By the gods, I have not:
Never did thought of mine levy offence;
Nor never did my actions yet commence
A deed might gain her love or your displeasure.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
By the gods, I have not:
Never did thought of mine levy offence;
Nor never did my actions yet commence
A deed might gain her love or your displeasure.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:

A deed might gain her love or your displeasure.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: Traitor, thou liest.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: Traitor!

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: Ay, traitor.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Even in his throat—unless it be the king—
That calls me traitor, I return the lie.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: [Aside] Now, by the gods, I do applaud his courage.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
My actions are as noble as my thoughts,
That never relish'd of a base descent.
I came unto your court for honour's cause,
And not to be a rebel to her state;
And he that otherwise accounts of me,
This sword shall prove he's honour's enemy.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
No?
Here comes my daughter, she can witness it.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
No?
Here comes my daughter, she can witness it.
Enter THAISA

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Then, as you are as virtuous as fair,
Resolve your angry father, if my tongue
Did ere solicit, or my hand subscribe
To any syllable that made love to you.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
Why, sir, say if you had,
Who takes offence at that would make me glad?

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Yea, mistress, are you so peremptory?
[Aside]
I am glad on't with all my heart.—
I'll tame you; I'll bring you in subjection.
Will you, not having my consent,
Bestow your love and your affections
Upon a stranger? who, for aught I know,
May be, nor can I think the contrary,
As great in blood as I myself.—
Therefore hear you, mistress; either frame
Your will to mine,— and you, sir, hear you,
Either be ruled by me, or I will make you—
Man and wife:
Nay, come, your hands and lips must seal it too:
And being join'd, I'll thus your hopes destroy;
And for a further grief,—God give you joy!—
What, are you both pleased?

Upon a stranger? who, for aught I know,
May be, nor can I think the contrary,
As great in blood as I myself.—
Therefore hear you, mistress; either frame
Your will to mine,— and you, sir, hear you,
Either be ruled by me, or I will make you—
Man and wife:
Nay, come, your hands and lips must seal it too:
And being join'd, I'll thus your hopes destroy;
And for a further grief,—God give you joy!—
What, are you both pleased?

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Yea, mistress, are you so peremptory?
[Aside]
I am glad on't with all my heart.—
I'll tame you; I'll bring you in subjection.
Will you, not having my consent,
Bestow your love and your affections
Upon a stranger? who, for aught I know,
May be, nor can I think the contrary,
As great in blood as I myself.—
Therefore hear you, mistress; either frame
Your will to mine,— and you, sir, hear you,
Either be ruled by me, or I will make you—
Man and wife:
Nay, come, your hands and lips must seal it too:
And being join'd, I'll thus your hopes destroy;
And for a further grief,—God give you joy!—
What, are you both pleased?

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
Yea, mistress, are you so peremptory?
[Aside]
I am glad on't with all my heart.—
I'll tame you; I'll bring you in subjection.

Therefore hear you, mistress; either frame
Your will to mine,— and you, sir, hear you,
Either be ruled by me, or I will make you—
Man and wife:
Nay, come, your hands and lips must seal it too:
And being join'd, I'll thus your hopes destroy;
And for a further grief,—God give you joy!—
What, are you both pleased?

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text: Yes, if you love me, sir.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: Even as my life, or blood that fosters it.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text: What, are you both agreed?

sentences.speaker: BOTH
sentences.text: Yes, if it please your majesty.

sentences.speaker: SIMONIDES
sentences.text:
It pleaseth me so well, that I will see you wed;
And then with what haste you can get you to bed.
Exeunt
Enter GOWER

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
Now sleep y-slaked hath the rout;
No din but snores the house about,
Made louder by the o'er-fed breast
Of this most pompous marriage-feast.
The cat, with eyne of burning coal,
Now crouches fore the mouse's hole;
And crickets sing at the oven's mouth,
E'er the blither for their drouth.
Hymen hath brought the bride to bed.
Where, by the loss of maidenhead,
A babe is moulded. Be attent,
And time that is so briefly spent
With your fine fancies quaintly eche:
What's dumb in show I'll plain with speech.

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
By many a dern and painful perch
Of Pericles the careful search,
By the four opposing coigns
Which the world together joins,
Is made with all due diligence
That horse and sail and high expense
Can stead the quest. At last from Tyre,
Fame answering the most strange inquire,
To the court of King Simonides
Are letters brought, the tenor these:
Antiochus and his daughter dead;
The men of Tyrus on the head
Of Helicanus would set on
The crown of Tyre, but he will none:
The mutiny he there hastes t' oppress;
Says to 'em, if King Pericles
Come not home in twice six moons,
He, obedient to their dooms,
Will take the crown. The sum of this,
Brought hither to Pentapolis,
Y-ravished the regions round,
And every one with claps can sound,
'Our heir-apparent is a king!
Who dream'd, who thought of such a thing?'
Brief, he must hence depart to Tyre:
His queen with child makes her desire—
Which who shall cross?— along to go:
Omit we all their dole and woe:
Lychorida, her nurse, she takes,
And so to sea. Their vessel shakes
On Neptune's billow; half the flood
Hath their keel cut: but fortune's mood
Varies again; the grisly north
Disgorges such a tempest forth,
That, as a duck for life that dives,
So up and down the poor ship drives:
The lady shrieks, and well-a-near
Does fall in travail with her fear:
And what ensues in this fell storm
Shall for itself itself perform.
I nill relate, action may
Conveniently the rest convey;
Which might not what by me is told.
In your imagination hold
This stage the ship, upon whose deck
The sea-tost Pericles appears to speak.

Hath their keel cut: but fortune's mood
Varies again; the grisly north
Disgorges such a tempest forth,
That, as a duck for life that dives,
So up and down the poor ship drives:
The lady shrieks, and well-a-near
Does fall in travail with her fear:
And what ensues in this fell storm
Shall for itself itself perform.
I nill relate, action may
Conveniently the rest convey;
Which might not what by me is told.
In your imagination hold
This stage the ship, upon whose deck
The sea-tost Pericles appears to speak.

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
By many a dern and painful perch
Of Pericles the careful search,
By the four opposing coigns
Which the world together joins,
Is made with all due diligence
That horse and sail and high expense
Can stead the quest. At last from Tyre,
Fame answering the most strange inquire,
To the court of King Simonides
Are letters brought, the tenor these:
Antiochus and his daughter dead;
The men of Tyrus on the head
Of Helicanus would set on
The crown of Tyre, but he will none:
The mutiny he there hastes t' oppress;
Says to 'em, if King Pericles
Come not home in twice six moons,
He, obedient to their dooms,
Will take the crown. The sum of this,
Brought hither to Pentapolis,
Y-ravished the regions round,
And every one with claps can sound,
'Our heir-apparent is a king!
Who dream'd, who thought of such a thing?'

His queen with child makes her desire—
Which who shall cross?— along to go:

Enter PERICLES, on shipboard

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Thou god of this great vast, rebuke these surges,
Which wash both heaven and hell; and thou, that hast
Upon the winds command, bind them in brass,
Having call'd them from the deep! O, still
Thy deafening, dreadful thunders; gently quench
Thy nimble, sulphurous flashes! O, how, Lychorida,
How does my queen? Thou stormest venomously;
Wilt thou spit all thyself? The seaman's whistle
Is as a whisper in the ears of death,
Unheard. Lychorida!— Lucina, O
Divinest patroness, and midwife gentle
To those that cry by night, convey thy deity
Aboard our dancing boat; make swift the pangs
Of my queen's travails!

Enter LYCHORIDA, with an Infant

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: Now, Lychorida!

sentences.speaker: LYCHORIDA
sentences.text:
Here is a thing too young for such a place,
Who, if it had conceit, would die, as I
Am like to do: take in your arms this piece
Of your dead queen.

sentences.speaker: LYCHORIDA
sentences.text:
Here is a thing too young for such a place,
Who, if it had conceit, would die, as I
Am like to do: take in your arms this piece
Of your dead queen.

sentences.speaker: LYCHORIDA
sentences.text:
Here is a thing too young for such a place,
Who, if it had conceit, would die, as I
Am like to do: take in your arms this piece
Of your dead queen.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: How, how, Lychorida!

sentences.speaker: LYCHORIDA
sentences.text:
Patience, good sir; do not assist the storm.
Here's all that is left living of your queen,
A little daughter: for the sake of it,
Be manly, and take comfort.

sentences.speaker: LYCHORIDA
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
O you gods!
Why do you make us love your goodly gifts,
And snatch them straight away? We here below
Recall not what we give, and therein may
Use honour with you.

sentences.speaker: LYCHORIDA
sentences.text:

Even for this charge.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Now, mild may be thy life!
For a more blustrous birth had never babe:
Quiet and gentle thy conditions! for
Thou art the rudeliest welcome to this world
That ever was prince's child. Happy what follows!
Thou hast as chiding a nativity
As fire, air, water, earth, and heaven can make,
To herald thee from the womb: even at the first
Thy loss is more than can thy portage quit,
With all thou canst find here. Now, the good gods
Throw their best eyes upon't!

Enter two Sailors

sentences.speaker: FIRST SAILOR
sentences.text: What courage, sir? God save you!

sentences.speaker: FIRST SAILOR
sentences.text: What courage, sir? God save you!

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Courage enough: I do not fear the flaw;
It hath done to me the worst. Yet, for the love
Of this poor infant, this fresh-new sea-farer,
I would it would be quiet.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: FIRST SAILOR
sentences.text:
Slack the bolins there! Thou wilt not, wilt thou?
Blow, and split thyself.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SAILOR

Blow, and split thyself.

Blow, and split thyself.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SAILOR
sentences.text:
But sea-room, an the brine and cloudy billow kiss
the moon, I care not.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SAILOR
sentences.text:
But sea-room, an the brine and cloudy billow kiss
the moon, I care not.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SAILOR
sentences.text:
Sir, your queen must overboard: the sea works high,
the wind is loud, and will not lie till the ship be
cleared of the dead.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SAILOR
sentences.text:

the wind is loud, and will not lie till the ship be
cleared of the dead.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: That's your superstition.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SAILOR
sentences.text:
Pardon us, sir; with us at sea it hath been still
observed: and we are strong in custom. Therefore
briefly yield her; for she must overboard straight.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SAILOR

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: As you think meet. Most wretched queen!

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: As you think meet. Most wretched queen!

sentences.speaker: LYCHORIDA
sentences.text: Here she lies, sir.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
A terrible childbed hast thou had, my dear;
No light, no fire: the unfriendly elements
Forgot thee utterly: nor have I time
To give thee hallow'd to thy grave, but straight
Must cast thee, scarcely coffin'd, in the ooze;
Where, for a monument upon thy bones,
And e'er-remaining lamps, the belching whale
And humming water must o'erwhelm thy corpse,
Lying with simple shells. O Lychorida,
Bid Nestor bring me spices, ink and paper,
My casket and my jewels; and bid Nicander
Bring me the satin coffer: lay the babe
Upon the pillow: hie thee, whiles I say
A priestly farewell to her: suddenly, woman.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SAILOR
sentences.text:
Sir, we have a chest beneath the hatches, caulked
and bitumed ready.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: I thank thee. Mariner, say what coast is this?

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: I thank thee. Mariner, say what coast is this?

sentences.speaker: SECOND SAILOR
sentences.text: We are near Tarsus.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Thither, gentle mariner.
Alter thy course for Tyre. When canst thou reach it?

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Thither, gentle mariner.
Alter thy course for Tyre. When canst thou reach it?

sentences.speaker: SECOND SAILOR
sentences.text: By break of day, if the wind cease.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
O, make for Tarsus!
There will I visit Cleon, for the babe
Cannot hold out to Tyrus: there I'll leave it
At careful nursing. Go thy ways, good mariner:
I'll bring the body presently.

Enter CERIMON, with a Servant, and some Persons who have been shipwrecked

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text: Philemon, ho!
Enter PHILEMON

sentences.speaker: PHILEMON
sentences.text: Doth my lord call?

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Get fire and meat for these poor men:
'T has been a turbulent and stormy night.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Get fire and meat for these poor men:
'T has been a turbulent and stormy night.

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text:
I have been in many; but such a night as this,
Till now, I ne'er endured.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Your master will be dead ere you return;
There's nothing can be minister'd to nature
That can recover him.
[TO PHILEMON]
Give this to the 'pothecary,
And tell me how it works.
Exeunt all but CERIMON
Enter two Gentlemen

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Good morrow.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Good morrow to your lordship.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Gentlemen,
Why do you stir so early?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Sir,
Our lodgings, standing bleak upon the sea,
Shook as the earth did quake;
The very principals did seem to rend,
And all-to topple: pure surprise and fear
Made me to quit the house.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Sir,
Our lodgings, standing bleak upon the sea,
Shook as the earth did quake;
The very principals did seem to rend,
And all-to topple: pure surprise and fear
Made me to quit the house.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Sir,
Our lodgings, standing bleak upon the sea,
Shook as the earth did quake;
The very principals did seem to rend,
And all-to topple: pure surprise and fear
Made me to quit the house.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
That is the cause we trouble you so early;
'Tis not our husbandry.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text: O, you say well.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
But I much marvel that your lordship, having
Rich tire about you, should at these early hours
Shake off the golden slumber of repose.
'Tis most strange,
Nature should be so conversant with pain,
Being thereto not compell'd.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
But I much marvel that your lordship, having
Rich tire about you, should at these early hours
Shake off the golden slumber of repose.
'Tis most strange,
Nature should be so conversant with pain,
Being thereto not compell'd.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
I hold it ever,
Virtue and cunning were endowments greater
Than nobleness and riches: careless heirs
May the two latter darken and expend;
But immortality attends the former.
Making a man a god. 'Tis known, I ever
Have studied physic, through which secret art,
By turning o'er authorities, I have,
Together with my practise, made familiar
To me and to my aid the blest infusions
That dwell in vegetives, in metals, stones;
And I can speak of the disturbances
That nature works, and of her cures; which doth give me
A more content in course of true delight
Than to be thirsty after tottering honour,
Or tie my treasure up in silken bags,
To please the fool and death.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
Your honour has through Ephesus pour'd forth
Your charity, and hundreds call themselves
Your creatures, who by you have been restored:
And not your knowledge, your personal pain, but even
Your purse, still open, hath built Lord Cerimon
Such strong renown as time shall ne'er decay.

Enter two or three Servants with a chest

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text: So; lift there.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text: What is that?

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
Sir, even now
Did the sea toss upon our shore this chest:
'Tis of some wreck.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text: Set 't down, let's look upon't.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: 'Tis like a coffin, sir.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Whate'er it be,
'Tis wondrous heavy. Wrench it open straight:
If the sea's stomach be o'ercharged with gold,
'Tis a good constraint of fortune it belches upon us.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: 'Tis so, my lord.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
How close 'tis caulk'd and bitumed!
Did the sea cast it up?

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
How close 'tis caulk'd and bitumed!
Did the sea cast it up?

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
I never saw so huge a billow, sir,
As toss'd it upon shore.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Wrench it open;
Soft! it smells most sweetly in my sense.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Wrench it open;
Soft! it smells most sweetly in my sense.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: A delicate odour.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
As ever hit my nostril. So, up with it.
O you most potent gods! what's here? a corse!

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
As ever hit my nostril. So, up with it.
O you most potent gods! what's here? a corse!

O you most potent gods! what's here? a corse!

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Most strange!

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Shrouded in cloth of state; balm'd and entreasured
With full bags of spices! A passport too!
Apollo, perfect me in the characters!
[Reading from a scroll]
'Here I give to understand,
If e'er this coffin drive a-land,
I, King Pericles, have lost
This queen, worth all our mundane cost.
Who finds her, give her burying;
She was the daughter of a king:
Besides this treasure for a fee,
The gods requite his charity!'
If thou livest, Pericles, thou hast a heart
That even cracks for woe! This chanced tonight.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Most likely, sir.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Nay, certainly to-night;
For look how fresh she looks! They were too rough
That threw her in the sea. Make a fire within:
Fetch hither all my boxes in my closet.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:

Fetch hither all my boxes in my closet.
Exit a Servant

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Death may usurp on nature many hours,
And yet the fire of life kindle again
The o'erpress'd spirits. I heard of an Egyptian
That had nine hours lien dead,
Who was by good appliance recovered.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:

And yet the fire of life kindle again
The o'erpress'd spirits. I heard of an Egyptian
That had nine hours lien dead,
Who was by good appliance recovered.
Re-enter a Servant, with boxes, napkins, and fire

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Well said, well said; the fire and cloths.
The rough and woeful music that we have,
Cause it to sound, beseech you.
The viol once more: how thou stirr'st, thou block!
The music there!— I pray you, give her air.
Gentlemen.
This queen will live: nature awakes; a warmth
Breathes out of her: she hath not been entranced
Above five hours: see how she gins to blow
Into life's flower again!

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
The heavens,
Through you, increase our wonder and set up
Your fame forever.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
The heavens,
Through you, increase our wonder and set up
Your fame forever.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
She is alive; behold,
Her eyelids, cases to those heavenly jewels
Which Pericles hath lost,
Begin to part their fringes of bright gold;
The diamonds of a most praised water
Do appear, to make the world twice rich. Live,
And make us weep to hear your fate, fair creature,
Rare as you seem to be.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
O dear Diana,
Where am I? Where's my lord? What world is this?

sentences.speaker: SECOND GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Is not this strange?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GENTLEMAN
sentences.text: Most rare.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Hush, my gentle neighbours!
Lend me your hands; to the next chamber bear her.
Get linen: now this matter must be look'd to,
For her relapse is mortal. Come, come;
And AEsculapius guide us!

Enter PERICLES, CLEON, DIONYZA, and LYCHORIDA with MARINA in her arms

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
Most honour'd Cleon, I must needs be gone;
My twelve months are expired, and Tyrus stands
In a litigious peace. You, and your lady,
Take from my heart all thankfulness! The gods
Make up the rest upon you!

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
Your shafts of fortune, though they hurt you mortally,
Yet glance full wanderingly on us.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
O your sweet queen!
That the strict fates had pleased you had brought her
hither,
To have bless'd mine eyes with her!

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
O your sweet queen!
That the strict fates had pleased you had brought her
hither,
To have bless'd mine eyes with her!

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
We cannot but obey
The powers above us. Could I rage and roar
As doth the sea she lies in, yet the end
Must be as 'tis. My gentle babe Marina, whom,
For she was born at sea, I have named so, here
I charge your charity withal, leaving her
The infant of your care; beseeching you
To give her princely training, that she may be
Manner'd as she is born.

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
Fear not, my lord, but think
Your grace, that fed my country with your corn,
For which the people's prayers still fall upon you,
Must in your child be thought on. If neglection
Should therein make me vile, the common body,
By you relieved, would force me to my duty:
But if to that my nature need a spur,
The gods revenge it upon me and mine,
To the end of generation!

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
I believe you;
Your honour and your goodness teach me to't,
Without your vows. Till she be married, madam,
By bright Diana, whom we honour, all
Unscissor'd shall this hair of mine remain,
Though I show ill in't. So I take my leave.
Good madam, make me blessed in your care
In bringing up my child.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
I have one myself,
Who shall not be more dear to my respect
Than yours, my lord.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
I have one myself,
Who shall not be more dear to my respect
Than yours, my lord.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text: Madam, my thanks and prayers.

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
We'll bring your grace e'en to the edge o' the shore,
Then give you up to the mask'd Neptune and
The gentlest winds of heaven.

sentences.speaker: PERICLES
sentences.text:
I will embrace
Your offer. Come, dearest madam. O, no tears,
Lychorida, no tears:
Look to your little mistress, on whose grace
You may depend hereafter. Come, my lord.

Enter CERIMON and THAISA

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Madam, this letter, and some certain jewels,
Lay with you in your coffer: which are now
At your command. Know you the character?

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Madam, this letter, and some certain jewels,
Lay with you in your coffer: which are now
At your command. Know you the character?

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Madam, this letter, and some certain jewels,
Lay with you in your coffer: which are now
At your command. Know you the character?

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
It is my lord's.
That I was shipp'd at sea, I well remember,
Even on my eaning time; but whether there
Deliver'd, by the holy gods,
I cannot rightly say. But since King Pericles,
My wedded lord, I ne'er shall see again,
A vestal livery will I take me to,
And never more have joy.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Madam, if this you purpose as ye speak,
Diana's temple is not distant far,
Where you may abide till your date expire.
Moreover, if you please, a niece of mine
Shall there attend you.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Madam, if this you purpose as ye speak,
Diana's temple is not distant far,
Where you may abide till your date expire.
Moreover, if you please, a niece of mine
Shall there attend you.

sentences.speaker: CERIMON
sentences.text:
Madam, if this you purpose as ye speak,

Moreover, if you please, a niece of mine

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
My recompense is thanks, that's all;
Yet my good will is great, though the gift small.

sentences.speaker: THAISA
sentences.text:
My recompense is thanks, that's all;
Yet my good will is great, though the gift small.
Exeunt
Enter GOWER

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
Imagine Pericles arrived at Tyre,
Welcomed and settled to his own desire.
His woeful queen we leave at Ephesus,
Unto Diana there a votaress.
Now to Marina bend your mind,
Whom our fast-growing scene must find
At Tarsus, and by Cleon train'd
In music, letters; who hath gain'd
Of education all the grace,
Which makes her both the heart and place
Of general wonder. But, alack,
That monster envy, oft the wrack
Of earned praise, Marina's life
Seeks to take off by treason's knife.
And in this kind hath our Cleon
One daughter, and a wench full grown,
Even ripe for marriage-rite; this maid
Hight Philoten: and it is said
For certain in our story, she
Would ever with Marina be:
Be't when she weaved the sleided silk
With fingers long, small, white as milk;
Or when she would with sharp needle wound
The cambric, which she made more sound
By hurting it; or when to the lute
She sung, and made the night-bird mute,
That still records with moan; or when
She would with rich and constant pen
Vail to her mistress Dian; still
This Philoten contends in skill
With absolute Marina: so
With the dove of Paphos might the crow
Vie feathers white. Marina gets
All praises, which are paid as debts,
And not as given. This so darks
In Philoten all graceful marks,
That Cleon's wife, with envy rare,
A present murderer does prepare
For good Marina, that her daughter
Might stand peerless by this slaughter.
The sooner her vile thoughts to stead,
Lychorida, our nurse, is dead:
And cursed Dionyza hath
The pregnant instrument of wrath
Prest for this blow. The unborn event
I do commend to your content:
Only I carry winged time
Post on the lame feet of my rhyme;
Which never could I so convey,
Unless your thoughts went on my way.
Dionyza does appear,
With Leonine, a murderer.

Vail to her mistress Dian; still

With absolute Marina: so

Vie feathers white. Marina gets
All praises, which are paid as debts,
And not as given. This so darks

Prest for this blow. The unborn event
I do commend to your content:

Post on the lame feet of my rhyme;
Which never could I so convey,
Unless your thoughts went on my way.

With Leonine, a murderer.

Exit
Enter DIONYZA and LEONINE

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
Thy oath remember; thou hast sworn to do't:
'Tis but a blow, which never shall be known.
Thou canst not do a thing in the world so soon,
To yield thee so much profit. Let not conscience,
Which is but cold, inflaming love i' thy bosom,
Inflame too nicely; nor let pity, which
Even women have cast off, melt thee, but be
A soldier to thy purpose.

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text: I will do't; but yet she is a goodly creature.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
The fitter, then, the gods should have her. Here
she comes weeping for her only mistress' death.
Thou art resolved?

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:

she comes weeping for her only mistress' death.
Thou art resolved?

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA

she comes weeping for her only mistress' death.
Thou art resolved?

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text: I am resolved.
Enter MARINA, with a basket of flowers

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
No, I will rob Tellus of her weed,
To strew thy green with flowers: the yellows, blues,
The purple violets, and marigolds,
Shall as a carpet hang upon thy grave,
While summer-days do last. Ay me! poor maid,
Born in a tempest, when my mother died,
This world to me is like a lasting storm,
Whirring me from my friends.

This world to me is like a lasting storm,
Whirring me from my friends.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
How now, Marina! why do you keep alone?
How chance my daughter is not with you? Do not
Consume your blood with sorrowing: you have
A nurse of me. Lord, how your favour's changed
With this unprofitable woe!
Come, give me your flowers, ere the sea mar it.
Walk with Leonine; the air is quick there,
And it pierces and sharpens the stomach. Come,
Leonine, take her by the arm, walk with her.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
No, I pray you;
I'll not bereave you of your servant.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
No, I pray you;
I'll not bereave you of your servant.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
Come, come;
I love the king your father, and yourself,
With more than foreign heart. We every day
Expect him here: when he shall come and find
Our paragon to all reports thus blasted,
He will repent the breadth of his great voyage;
Blame both my lord and me, that we have taken
No care to your best courses. Go, I pray you,
Walk, and be cheerful once again; reserve
That excellent complexion, which did steal
The eyes of young and old. Care not for me
I can go home alone.

sentences.speaker: MARINA

Well, I will go;
But yet I have no desire to it.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
Come, come, I know 'tis good for you.
Walk half an hour, Leonine, at the least:
Remember what I have said.

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text: I warrant you, madam.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
I'll leave you, my sweet lady, for a while:
Pray, walk softly, do not heat your blood:
What! I must have a care of you.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:

What! I must have a care of you.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: My thanks, sweet madam.
Exit DIONYZA

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: Is this wind westerly that blows?

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text: South-west.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: When I was born, the wind was north.

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text: Was't so?

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
My father, as nurse said, did never fear,
But cried 'Good seaman!' to the sailors, galling
His kingly hands, haling ropes;
And, clasping to the mast, endured a sea
That almost burst the deck.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
My father, as nurse said, did never fear,
But cried 'Good seaman!' to the sailors, galling
His kingly hands, haling ropes;
And, clasping to the mast, endured a sea
That almost burst the deck.

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text: When was this?

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
When I was born:
Never was waves nor wind more violent;
And from the ladder-tackle washes off
A canvas-climber. 'Ha!' says one, 'wilt out?'
And with a dropping industry they skip
From stem to stern: the boatswain whistles, and
The master calls, and trebles their confusion.

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text: Come, say your prayers.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: What mean you?

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text:
If you require a little space for prayer,
I grant it: pray; but be not tedious,
For the gods are quick of ear, and I am sworn
To do my work with haste.

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text:
If you require a little space for prayer,
I grant it: pray; but be not tedious,
For the gods are quick of ear, and I am sworn
To do my work with haste.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: Why will you kill me?

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text: To satisfy my lady.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
Why would she have me kill'd?
Now, as I can remember, by my troth,
I never did her hurt in all my life:
I never spake bad word, nor did ill turn
To any living creature: believe me, la,
I never kill'd a mouse, nor hurt a fly:
I trod upon a worm against my will,
But I wept for it. How have I offended,
Wherein my death might yield her any profit,
Or my life imply her any danger?

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text:
My commission
Is not to reason of the deed, but do it.

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text:
My commission
Is not to reason of the deed, but do it.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
You will not do't for all the world, I hope.
You are well favour'd, and your looks foreshow
You have a gentle heart. I saw you lately,
When you caught hurt in parting two that fought:
Good sooth, it show'd well in you: do so now:
Your lady seeks my life; come you between,
And save poor me, the weaker.

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text:
I am sworn,
And will dispatch.
[He seizes her]
Enter Pirates

sentences.speaker: FIRST PIRATE
sentences.text: Hold, villain!
LEONINE runs away

sentences.speaker: SECOND PIRATE
sentences.text: A prize! a prize!

sentences.speaker: SECOND PIRATE
sentences.text: A prize! a prize!

sentences.speaker: THIRD PIRATE
sentences.text:
Half-part, mates, half-part.
Come, let's have her aboard suddenly.

sentences.speaker: THIRD PIRATE
sentences.text:
Half-part, mates, half-part.
Come, let's have her aboard suddenly.
Exeunt Pirates with MARINA
Re-enter LEONINE

sentences.speaker: LEONINE
sentences.text:
These roguing thieves serve the great pirate Valdes;
And they have seized Marina. Let her go:
There's no hope she will return. I'll swear
she's dead,
And thrown into the sea. But I'll see further:
Perhaps they will but please themselves upon her,
Not carry her aboard. If she remain,
Whom they have ravish'd must by me be slain.

Enter Pandar, Bawd, and BOULT

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text: Boult!

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text: Sir?

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Search the market narrowly; Mytilene is full of
gallants. We lost too much money this mart by being
too wenchless.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Search the market narrowly; Mytilene is full of
gallants. We lost too much money this mart by being
too wenchless.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Search the market narrowly; Mytilene is full of
gallants. We lost too much money this mart by being
too wenchless.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
We were never so much out of creatures. We have but
poor three, and they can do no more than they can
do; and they with continual action are even as good as
rotten.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
We were never so much out of creatures. We have but
poor three, and they can do no more than they can
do; and they with continual action are even as good as
rotten.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
We were never so much out of creatures. We have but
poor three, and they can do no more than they can
do; and they with continual action are even as good as
rotten.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Therefore let's have fresh ones, whate'er we pay for
them. If there be not a conscience to be used in
every trade, we shall never prosper.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Therefore let's have fresh ones, whate'er we pay for
them. If there be not a conscience to be used in
every trade, we shall never prosper.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Thou sayest true: 'tis not our bringing up of poor
bastards,—as, I think, I have brought up some eleven—

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Thou sayest true: 'tis not our bringing up of poor
bastards,—as, I think, I have brought up some eleven—

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Thou sayest true: 'tis not our bringing up of poor
bastards,—as, I think, I have brought up some eleven—

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Ay, to eleven; and brought them down again. But
shall I search the market?

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Ay, to eleven; and brought them down again. But
shall I search the market?

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
What else, man? The stuff we have, a strong wind
will blow it to pieces, they are so pitifully sodden.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
What else, man? The stuff we have, a strong wind
will blow it to pieces, they are so pitifully sodden.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Thou sayest true; they're too unwholesome, o'
conscience. The poor Transylvanian is dead, that
lay with the little baggage.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Thou sayest true; they're too unwholesome, o'
conscience. The poor Transylvanian is dead, that
lay with the little baggage.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Ay, she quickly pooped him; she made him roast-meat
for worms. But I'll go search the market.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Ay, she quickly pooped him; she made him roast-meat
for worms. But I'll go search the market.
Exit

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Three or four thousand chequins were as pretty a
proportion to live quietly, and so give over.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Why to give over, I pray you? is it a shame to get
when we are old?

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Why to give over, I pray you? is it a shame to get
when we are old?

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
O, our credit comes not in like the commodity, nor
the commodity wages not with the danger: therefore,
if in our youths we could pick up some pretty
estate, 'twere not amiss to keep our door hatched.
Besides, the sore terms we stand upon with the gods
will be strong with us for giving over.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:

Besides, the sore terms we stand upon with the gods

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Come, other sorts offend as well as we.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Come, other sorts offend as well as we.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
As well as we! ay, and better too; we offend worse.
Neither is our profession any trade; it's no
calling. But here comes Boult.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
As well as we! ay, and better too; we offend worse.
Neither is our profession any trade; it's no
calling. But here comes Boult.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:

Neither is our profession any trade; it's no
calling. But here comes Boult.
Re-enter BOULT, with the Pirates and MARINA

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
[To MARINA] Come your ways. My masters, you say
she's a virgin?

sentences.speaker: FIRST PIRATE
sentences.text: O, sir, we doubt it not.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Master, I have gone through for this piece, you see:
if you like her, so; if not, I have lost my earnest.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Master, I have gone through for this piece, you see:
if you like her, so; if not, I have lost my earnest.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Master, I have gone through for this piece, you see:
if you like her, so; if not, I have lost my earnest.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Boult, has she any qualities?

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
She has a good face, speaks well, and has excellent
good clothes: there's no further necessity of
qualities can make her be refused.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
She has a good face, speaks well, and has excellent
good clothes: there's no further necessity of
qualities can make her be refused.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: What's her price, Boult?

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text: I cannot be bated one doit of a thousand pieces.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Well, follow me, my masters, you shall have your
money presently. Wife, take her in; instruct her
what she has to do, that she may not be raw in her
entertainment.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Well, follow me, my masters, you shall have your
money presently. Wife, take her in; instruct her
what she has to do, that she may not be raw in her
entertainment.

sentences.speaker: PANDAR
sentences.text:
Well, follow me, my masters, you shall have your
money presently. Wife, take her in; instruct her
what she has to do, that she may not be raw in her
entertainment.
Exeunt Pandar and Pirates

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Boult, take you the marks of her, the colour of her
hair, complexion, height, age, with warrant of her
virginity; and cry 'He that will give most shall
have her first.' Such a maidenhead were no cheap
thing, if men were as they have been. Get this done
as I command you.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Boult, take you the marks of her, the colour of her
hair, complexion, height, age, with warrant of her
virginity; and cry 'He that will give most shall
have her first.' Such a maidenhead were no cheap
thing, if men were as they have been. Get this done
as I command you.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text: Performance shall follow.
Exit

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
Alack that Leonine was so slack, so slow!
He should have struck, not spoke; or that these
pirates,
Not enough barbarous, had not o'erboard thrown me
For to seek my mother!

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:

pirates,
Not enough barbarous, had not o'erboard thrown me
For to seek my mother!

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:

pirates,

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Why lament you, pretty one?

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: That I am pretty.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Come, the gods have done their part in you.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: I accuse them not.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
You are light into my hands, where you are like to
live.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
The more my fault
To scape his hands where I was like to die.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Ay, and you shall live in pleasure.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: No.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Yes, indeed shall you, and taste gentlemen of all
fashions: you shall fare well; you shall have the
difference of all complexions. What! do you stop your
ears?

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Yes, indeed shall you, and taste gentlemen of all
fashions: you shall fare well; you shall have the
difference of all complexions. What! do you stop your
ears?

ears?

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Yes, indeed shall you, and taste gentlemen of all
fashions: you shall fare well; you shall have the
difference of all complexions. What! do you stop your
ears?

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: Are you a woman?

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: What would you have me be, an I be not a woman?

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: An honest woman, or not a woman.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Marry, whip thee, gosling: I think I shall have
something to do with you. Come, you're a young
foolish sapling, and must be bowed as I would have
you.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Marry, whip thee, gosling: I think I shall have
something to do with you. Come, you're a young
foolish sapling, and must be bowed as I would have
you.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:

something to do with you. Come, you're a young
foolish sapling, and must be bowed as I would have
you.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: The gods defend me!

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
If it please the gods to defend you by men, then men
must comfort you, men must feed you, men must stir
you up. Boult's returned.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
If it please the gods to defend you by men, then men
must comfort you, men must feed you, men must stir
you up. Boult's returned.
Re-enter BOULT

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Now, sir, hast thou cried her through the market?

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
I have cried her almost to the number of her hairs;
I have drawn her picture with my voice.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
I have cried her almost to the number of her hairs;
I have drawn her picture with my voice.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
And I prithee tell me, how dost thou find the
inclination of the people, especially of the younger
sort?

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
'Faith, they listened to me as they would have
hearkened to their father's testament. There was a
Spaniard's mouth so watered, that he went to bed to
her very description.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
'Faith, they listened to me as they would have
hearkened to their father's testament. There was a
Spaniard's mouth so watered, that he went to bed to
her very description.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: We shall have him here to-morrow with his best ruff on.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
To-night, to-night. But, mistress, do you know the
French knight that cowers i' the hams?

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
To-night, to-night. But, mistress, do you know the
French knight that cowers i' the hams?

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Who, Monsieur Veroles?

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Who, Monsieur Veroles?

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Ay, he: he offered to cut a caper at the
proclamation; but he made a groan at it, and swore
he would see her to-morrow.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Ay, he: he offered to cut a caper at the
proclamation; but he made a groan at it, and swore
he would see her to-morrow.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Well, well; as for him, he brought his disease
hither: here he does but repair it. I know he will
come in our shadow, to scatter his crowns in the
sun.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Well, well; as for him, he brought his disease
hither: here he does but repair it. I know he will
come in our shadow, to scatter his crowns in the
sun.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
Well, if we had of every nation a traveller, we
should lodge them with this sign.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
[To MARINA] Pray you, come hither awhile. You
have fortunes coming upon you. Mark me: you must
seem to do that fearfully which you commit
willingly, despise profit where you have most gain.
To weep that you live as ye do makes pity in your
lovers: seldom but that pity begets you a good
opinion, and that opinion a mere profit.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text: I understand you not.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
O, take her home, mistress, take her home: these
blushes of hers must be quenched with some present
practise.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
O, take her home, mistress, take her home: these
blushes of hers must be quenched with some present
practise.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Thou sayest true, i' faith, so they must; for your
bride goes to that with shame which is her way to go
with warrant.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Thou sayest true, i' faith, so they must; for your
bride goes to that with shame which is her way to go
with warrant.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
'Faith, some do, and some do not. But, mistress, if
I have bargained for the joint,—

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
'Faith, some do, and some do not. But, mistress, if
I have bargained for the joint,—

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Thou mayst cut a morsel off the spit.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text: I may so.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Who should deny it? Come, young one, I like the
manner of your garments well.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Who should deny it? Come, young one, I like the
manner of your garments well.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text: Ay, by my faith, they shall not be changed yet.

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
Boult, spend thou that in the town: report what a
sojourner we have; you'll lose nothing by custom.
When nature flamed this piece, she meant thee a good
turn; therefore say what a paragon she is, and thou
hast the harvest out of thine own report.

sentences.text:

sojourner we have; you'll lose nothing by custom.

turn; therefore say what a paragon she is, and thou
hast the harvest out of thine own report.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
I warrant you, mistress, thunder shall not so awake
the beds of eels as my giving out her beauty stir up
the lewdly-inclined. I'll bring home some to-night.

sentences.speaker: BOULT
sentences.text:
I warrant you, mistress, thunder shall not so awake

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text: Come your ways; follow me.

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
If fires be hot, knives sharp, or waters deep,
Untied I still my virgin knot will keep.
Diana, aid my purpose!

sentences.speaker: MARINA
sentences.text:
If fires be hot, knives sharp, or waters deep,
Untied I still my virgin knot will keep.
Diana, aid my purpose!

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
What have we to do with Diana? Pray you, will you go
with us?

sentences.speaker: BAWD
sentences.text:
What have we to do with Diana? Pray you, will you go
with us?
Exeunt
Enter CLEON and DIONYZA

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text: Why, are you foolish? Can it be undone?

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
O Dionyza, such a piece of slaughter
The sun and moon ne'er look'd upon!

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
I think
You'll turn a child again.

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
Were I chief lord of all this spacious world,
I'ld give it to undo the deed. O lady,
Much less in blood than virtue, yet a princess
To equal any single crown o' the earth
I' the justice of compare! O villain Leonine!
Whom thou hast poison'd too:
If thou hadst drunk to him, 't had been a kindness
Becoming well thy fact: what canst thou say
When noble Pericles shall demand his child?

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
That she is dead. Nurses are not the fates,
To foster it, nor ever to preserve.
She died at night; I'll say so. Who can cross it?
Unless you play the pious innocent,
And for an honest attribute cry out
'She died by foul play.'

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
O, go to. Well, well,
Of all the faults beneath the heavens, the gods
Do like this worst.

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
O, go to. Well, well,
Of all the faults beneath the heavens, the gods
Do like this worst.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
Be one of those that think
The petty wrens of Tarsus will fly hence,
And open this to Pericles. I do shame
To think of what a noble strain you are,
And of how coward a spirit.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
Be one of those that think
The petty wrens of Tarsus will fly hence,
And open this to Pericles. I do shame
To think of what a noble strain you are,
And of how coward a spirit.

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
To such proceeding
Who ever but his approbation added,
Though not his prime consent, he did not flow
From honourable sources.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
Be it so, then:
Yet none does know, but you, how she came dead,
Nor none can know, Leonine being gone.
She did disdain my child, and stood between
Her and her fortunes: none would look on her,
But cast their gazes on Marina's face;
Whilst ours was blurted at and held a malkin
Not worth the time of day. It pierced me through;
And though you call my course unnatural,
You not your child well loving, yet I find
It greets me as an enterprise of kindness
Perform'd to your sole daughter.

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text: Heavens forgive it!

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
And as for Pericles,
What should he say? We wept after her hearse,
And yet we mourn: her monument
Is almost finish'd, and her epitaphs
In glittering golden characters express
A general praise to her, and care in us
At whose expense 'tis done.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:

And yet we mourn: her monument
Is almost finish'd, and her epitaphs
In glittering golden characters express
A general praise to her, and care in us
At whose expense 'tis done.

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
Thou art like the harpy,
Which, to betray, dost, with thine angel's face,
Seize with thine eagle's talons.

sentences.speaker: CLEON
sentences.text:
Thou art like the harpy,
Which, to betray, dost, with thine angel's face,
Seize with thine eagle's talons.

sentences.speaker: DIONYZA
sentences.text:
You are like one that superstitiously
Doth swear to the gods that winter kills the flies:
But yet I know you'll do as I advise.
Exeunt
Enter GOWER, before the monument of MARINA at Tarsus

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
Thus time we waste, and longest leagues make short;
Sail seas in cockles, have an wish but for't;
Making, to take your imagination,
From bourn to bourn, region to region.
By you being pardon'd, we commit no crime
To use one language in each several clime
Where our scenes seem to live. I do beseech you
To learn of me, who stand i' the gaps to teach you,
The stages of our story. Pericles
Is now again thwarting the wayward seas,
Attended on by many a lord and knight.
To see his daughter, all his life's delight.
Old Escanes, whom Helicanus late
Advanced in time to great and high estate,
Is left to govern. Bear you it in mind,
Old Helicanus goes along behind.
Well-sailing ships and bounteous winds have brought
This king to Tarsus,— think his pilot thought;
So with his steerage shall your thoughts grow on,—
To fetch his daughter home, who first is gone.
Like motes and shadows see them move awhile;
Your ears unto your eyes I'll reconcile.

sentences.speaker: GOWER
sentences.text:
See how belief may suffer by foul show!
This borrow'd passion stands for true old woe;
And Pericles, in sorrow all devour'd,
With sighs shot through, and biggest tears
o'ershower'd,
Leaves Tarsus and again embarks.
He swears
Never to wash his face, nor cut his hairs:
